nativeRange.habitat: Shallow to subtidal reef zones on rocky and rubble substrate; photic zone

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product photo from Mosaic Macros listing — sole hobby source for this species

summary: Flat-Branched Pink Thicket (Dichotomaria marginata) is a rare calcareous red macroalgae with a reddish-pink, flat-branched thallus and distinctive white tips, growing in a compact spherical form. Seldom available outside specialty sources; generally considered an advanced-care species.
careNotes:
Flat-Branched Pink Thicket is a calcareous red macroalgae in the genus Dichotomaria (family Galaxauraceae, order Nemaliales, class Florideophyceae, division Rhodophyta). All members of Galaxauraceae are calcified — they deposit calcium carbonate into their thallus (the plant body) — making this species morphologically and physiologically similar to its family relatives Galaxaura sp. ('Bramble Thicket') and Galaxaura rugosa ('Tubular Thicket'). The thallus of D. marginata is reddish-pink with flat, dichotomously branching fronds (each branch splitting into two equal forks) adorned with distinctive white tips, growing in a roughly spherical form. This flat-branched morphology distinguishes it from the fuzzy-filamentous Galaxaura species in the same family.

As a calcified alga, stable calcium (around 400–450 ppm) and magnesium (around 1250–1350 ppm) levels are generally advised, consistent with a well-maintained reef system. No species-specific aquarium data exist for D. marginata, so these targets are drawn from family-level Galaxauraceae husbandry. Standard reef parameters (SG 1.023–1.026, temperature 24–27°C, pH 8.1–8.4, kH 8–12 dKH) are recommended. High light intensity tends to be beneficial for calcified red macroalgae with this growth form; placement in well-lit areas of the display tank is commonly advised. Growth is generally slow, consistent with the metabolic demands of skeletal calcification. Propagation is typically accomplished by fragmenting the branching thallus.

Dichotomaria is a cosmopolitan genus, so the exact regional provenance of aquarium specimens may vary from the Caribbean distribution most commonly cited. This species is seldom available outside specialty macroalgae retailers, where it is sold as a display specimen. Related display specimens from the same family include Galaxaura sp. ('Bramble Thicket') and Galaxaura rugosa ('Tubular Thicket'), which share similar calcified morphology and reef husbandry requirements.

DO NOT bury this alga in sand — as a red macroalgae, it will MELT at the base if the lower thallus is covered. Attach it to rock using superglue, zip-tie, fishing line, or by wedging it into a crevice instead. Sand placement is generally suitable only for genera such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, and some calcified Dasycladales.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source. Confirmed scientific name Dichotomaria marginata, trade name, and morphology (flat-branched, reddish-pink thallus with white tips, spherical growth form). Minimal husbandry parameters on product page.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Galaxauraceae
additional.notes: Confirmed family Galaxauraceae, order Nemaliales, class Florideophyceae, division Rhodophyta. Confirmed Dichotomaria as one of four accepted genera in the family (20 species). All family members are calcified. Cosmopolitan marine distribution. Used for taxonomy verification and family-level husbandry extrapolation — no species-specific aquarium data.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
